title: M28 Region / sector modifier primitive
summary: Generic 'a rule that applies to entities inside a region' primitive. Components: `Region { shape: rect | polygon | disc, layer? }` + `RegionTag` (which entities are affected) + `RegionRule` (what changes inside the region — fuse-time multiplier, gravity scale, damage mask, minimap visibility, etc.). Required by Kaboom Crew's sector modifiers (fast-fuse zones, reactor reflect zones, storm-disables-minimap zones — see §14 of the readiness analysis) and the network-relevance / fog-of-war queries that need exactly the same shape primitives. Project games declare specific rules; the engine ships the region-membership query + the tag-injection system.
doneCriteria: Engine `RegionMembershipSystem` maintains a sparse `entity → region[]` index updated on Transform/Region change.
`runtime.queryRegion(regionId)` returns the entities currently inside — Pickable / Networked / VFX systems consume it without re-deriving membership.
Doctor warns on overlapping region layers + per-frame region-query cost.
Kaboom Crew level uses two distinct regions (fast-fuse + reflect).
notes: Source: §14 of notes/dynabomber-readiness-analysis.md (consolidates the sector-modifier and run-time-mutation rows).
Sister epic for fog-of-war / network relevance: same region primitive, different consumers — explicit goal is to NOT build three near-identical region implementations.
